threads.label: Iran / Hormuz
threads.status_line: Deal NOT signed on Trump's 80th birthday (Jun 14); Iran called Sunday date a 'propaganda event'; text agreed, Khamenei approved, Pakistan confirmed final language; Brent $87; signing imminent in coming days; MOU only 'conceptually' addresses uranium stockpiles; hardliners attacking draft

Khamenei rejects deal text publicly

source_discoveries.name: Richard Haass — 'Home & Away' Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Former CFR president arguing Trump is recreating the JCPOA he destroyed, and that regime change pressure produced a more hardline Iranian government — structural counter-frame to deal optimism

source_discoveries.name: L'Orient Today
source_discoveries.why_interesting: English-language edition of L'Orient Le Jour; takes a meaningfully different political line from Al-Nahar on Lebanese politics; useful for south Lebanon coverage

source_discoveries.name: Asharq Al-Awsat (English)
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Gulf-backed pan-Arab daily; broke the Syria parliament June 8 story before other outlets; consistently ahead on Gulf/Arab political transitions and has senior source access in Beirut and Damascus

source_discoveries.name: Velina Tchakarova — Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Vienna-based geopolitical analyst; Austrian/Central European perspective on Ukraine distinct from Washington/London axis; published 'The Ukraine War: The June Opening and Its Limits' Jun 4 — structural frame less invested in Ukrainian optimism or Western hand-wringing

source_discoveries.name: Alastair Crooke — Conflicts Forum Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Former senior British diplomat and MI6 officer; argues Hezbollah advises Iran rather than takes orders, inverting the proxy narrative; Jun 2026 pieces frame Iran's posture as calculated 'escalatory deterrence' not desperate deal-seeking — 'America's Suez Crisis' thesis argues US is in Britain's 1956 position; prediction confirmed within 24h on Jun 11

source_discoveries.name: Trita Parsi — Quincy Institute / Responsible Statecraft
source_discoveries.why_interesting: NIAC founder, Quincy Institute fellow; consistent frame since April that Trump is 'not in a dominant position' and that the fire-for-fire exchange is bounded by both sides' inability to afford full escalation; structurally different from both hawkish and catastrophist Western readings; Jun 12 'Warmongers in meltdown' piece names the Washington institutional battle most clearly

source_discoveries.name: Foundation for Defense of Democracies (FDD)
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Hawkish Washington think tank; published IAEA non-compliance analysis on Jun 12 at same moment Trump claimed deal — explicitly building the legal/institutional case for rejecting any Iran deal as insufficient; useful as real-time counter-weight to Quincy Institute framing; tracks closely with Netanyahu's likely response

source_discoveries.name: Hamidreza Azizi — SWP Berlin
source_discoveries.why_interesting: German-Iranian scholar at the German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP); noted that Iranian commentators are openly treating the birthday timing as a significant factor in deal delay — insider read on Iranian domestic discourse not available in Western press; cited by Fortune June 13
